calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
Off Calendar Aug-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
David J. Cook, Esq. 415-989-4730 415-989-0491 Cook Collection Attorneys PLC for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. Mr. Cook states he is not contesting the court's tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PERFORMANCE FOOD GROUP, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order.

Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc.'s motion for assignment of rights, restraining order and turnover order, for which no opposition was received, is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

The moving papers are clearly not related to this action. Simply put, the judgment creditor here does not "provid[e] invisible braces and other orthodontic materials to buyers" and the judgment debtor is not a "dental practic[e]." (See Compl., filed Oct. 16, 2024.) Suffice it to say the motion is based on inapposite facts. If the motion is resubmitted the facts and the legal discussion must relate to this case.

This is not the first time counsel has submitted nonsensical papers. (See Align Tech., Inc., v. USA Dental, S.F, Super. Court Case No. CGC-24-618007.) Counsel is admonished to ensure papers submitted to the court satisfy legal and ethical standards.

Additionally, the court has previously instructed counsel to timely submit a coherent proposed order that is consistent with the motion. (See Align Tech., Inc., v. Gulia Omene DMD LLC, S.F, Super. Court Case No. CGC-24-618850.) The court clarifies counsel should submit a proposed order with the moving papers, if he wants to avoid delay or an order taking the motion off calendar.

To be clear, the court has a keen interest in enforcement of its judgments. It also has a keen interest in due process and fair play.

No later than 8:30 am on August 15, 2025, Moving Party is ordered to lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim send the proposed order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 585(A)(B)
Off Calendar Jul-11-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was set for Prove- Up for a Request for Default Judgment against Defendant The Napa Dell WC, LLC a Limited Liability Company, DBA the Napa Dell and Defendant David Barraza, an individual.

Dept: 505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K.Valdes; Bailiff: Deputy Guitron; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at a.m. and concluding at a.m.(D505)

David Cook, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff Performance Food Group, Nc. A Corporation.
Defendant The Napa Dell WC, LLC a Limited Liability Company, DBA the Napa Dell and Defendant David Barraza, An Individual not appearing.
Cristin Garden, sworn to testify for the Plaintiff.

Plaintiff's complaint filed Oct. 15, 2024.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendants filed Oct. 22, 2024.
Dismissal of Does filed on Mar. 11, 2025.
Default entered as to Defendants on Mar. 11, 2025.

The Court examines the witness. Witness testifies as to the declarations filed and proof to the request for damages.

Counsel states that declarations and statements made are true and correct.
The court denies the request for pre-judgment interest and does not find enough evidence to support the request.

Good cause appearing, the Court finds that the Plaintiff has proven its prima facie case and rules in favor of the Plaintiff against the Defendants as follows:

The Plaintiff shall recover from the Defendants:
Damages $16,138.59
Pre-Judgment Interest at 10% $0
Costs $0
Attorney138's Fees $500.00
Other $0
Total Judgment $16,638.59

Proposed judgment as modified signed and executed this day.

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 585(A)(B)
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Continued To Jul-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Deputies Gasper and Brown; Dept. 505
Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:56 a.m. and concluding at 11:03 a.m.

Plaintiff Performance Food Group appearing represented by David Julian Cook, Esq., testifies.
Witness for the Plaintiff Stephanie Wilson appeared via zoom.
Defendant The Napa Deli not appearing.

The court finds good cause to continue this matter to July 11, 2025 at 9:00am as Plaintiff's witness Ms. Wilson's zoom connection was eratic and not stable.

Plaintiff is encouraged to have all parties to this action appear in person at the next hearing.

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 585(A)(B)
